title: HPF-FS of Solarion arienae for transmission electron microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3b708","text":"This is an optimized version of the protocol for standard HPF-FS fixation for transmission electron microscopy that was used for the fixation of the culture of Solarion arienae.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":160,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge a well-grown culture at 1500 g at 4 °C.
After high-pressure freezing, the samples were transferred to precooled (-90 °C) fixative medium (2% OsO4 in 100% acetone) and processed by automatic freeze substitution (Leica EM AFS), as follows:
Cut sections. For Solarion HPF-FS , 90nm thick sections were cut with a diamond knife on an an EM UC6 (Leica) ultramicrotome.
post-contrast with uranyl acetate and lead citrate.
The obtained pellet was cryo-immobilized by high-pressure freezing (Leica EM ICE) in gold-plated, 3 mm wide copper carriers with a cavity of 0.1 mm (Wohlwend GmbH).
After AFS, the samples were transferred to room temperature and washed with 100% acetone (3x).
After washing, infiltrate sequentially for 1h each with:
Finally, the samples were infiltrated with pure resin overnight (EMbed 812)
Lastly, coat with a carbon layer.
creator: Marek Valt
